channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tanzania Government Introduces Diesel Subsidy Following Fuel Price Increases
item.description: The Tanzania government has taken action to support citizens as fuel prices continue to rise sharply. According to EWURA, the new retail price for petrol in Dar es Salaam is now 4,115 shillings per li...

item.title: Legal Sector Code Introduced to Enhance Black Ownership and Inclusion in South African Law Firms
item.description: The South African government has introduced a new Legal Sector Code (LSC), aiming to accelerate transformation in the country's legal profession. Gazetted in September 2024 by Trade, Industry and Comp...

item.title: India’s cultural presence stands out at Met Gala 2026 amid debates on billionaire patronage
item.description: The Met Gala 2026 unfolded with a mix of artistic celebration and underlying controversy, particularly around the involvement of billionaire patronage led by Jeff Bezos. While the event raised a recor...

item.title: WBBSE Class 10 Result 2026 to Be Declared on May 8: Details on Marksheet, Timing, and Passing Criteria
item.description: The West Bengal Board of Secondary Education (WBBSE) is set to announce the Class 10 Madhyamik results for the year 2026 on May 8. Students awaiting their results can access their mark sheets online t...

item.title: Federal government settles class-action for $8.7M after 2020 CRA account cyberattacks
item.description: The Canadian federal government has agreed to a settlement of $8.7 million for a class-action lawsuit stemming from cyberattacks in 2020 that affected tens of thousands of Canadians. Hackers targeted ...

item.title: Manitoba MP pushes for national Silver Alert system to protect missing seniors
item.description: A private member’s bill proposing a national Silver Alert system, similar to Amber Alerts for children, is moving forward in the House of Commons. Conservative MP Raquel Dancho, representing Kildonan-...

item.title: Fans Discuss Beyoncé’s Appearance at Met Gala Amid Blue Ivy’s Debut
item.description: Beyoncé’s appearance at the Met Gala on May 4, 2026, created a buzz among fans and online observers, mainly due to perceptions of her ageing. The singer and style icon wore a dramatic crystal-embellis...

item.title: Understanding Why AI Chatbots Appear Conscious, But Are Not
item.description: Recently, evolutionary biologist Richard Dawkins penned an op-ed suggesting that AI chatbot Claude might exhibit signs of consciousness. While Dawkins did not claim certainty, he noted that the chatbo...

item.title: DG ISPR Details Pakistan's Indigenous Military Strength and Strategic Preparedness
item.description: On May 7, 2026, DG ISPR Lt Gen Ahmed Sharif Chaudhry, during a press conference in Islamabad, highlighted Pakistan's indigenous military capabilities while marking the first anniversary of the 2025 co...

item.title: BJP Sends Legal Notice to Punjab CM Bhagwant Mann Over His Remarks on Punjab Blasts
item.description: The Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) national general secretary, Tarun Chugh, has sent a legal notice to Punjab Chief Minister Bhagwant Mann, demanding a public apology for his comments allegedly linking ...

item.title: Dale Hayes sets European Tour record at Italian Open
item.description: On this day in South African sporting history, several notable events stand out. In 1960, Durban-born Des Horne became the second South African to lift the FA Cup, guiding Wolverhampton Wanderers to a...

item.title: Trump Pushes for Deal with Iran Amid Tensions Over Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Tensions continue to escalate in the Middle East as the Iran-Israel conflict shows no signs of immediate resolution. On May 6, 2026, U.S. President Donald Trump expressed optimism about reaching a pos...

item.title: Quezon City court denies petition seeking to halt impeachment proceedings against Duterte
item.description: A Quezon City court has dismissed a petition that aimed to stop the ongoing impeachment proceedings involving Duterte, allowing the legal and political process to move forward. Based on the available ...

item.title: Soweto Fashion Week Highlights Emerging and Established Designers
item.description: The 15th annual Soweto Fashion Week (SFW) showcased a vibrant mix of rising stars and established fashion names, confirming its status as a key platform for emerging designers. Held over three days at...

item.title: International health authorities trace hantavirus exposure linked to cruise ship outbreak
item.description: Health authorities across several countries are working to trace potential exposure to a rare hantavirus strain following an outbreak aboard the cruise ship MV Hondius. The virus, believed to be the A...

item.title: AIADMK shifts MLAs to Puducherry amid government formation talks in Tamil Nadu
item.description: The AIADMK has relocated its newly elected MLAs to neighbouring Puducherry, a move reminiscent of resort politics, according to party sources. AIADMK spokesperson Kovai Sathyan confirmed that legislat...

item.title: Emirates Group Posts Profit Growth Amid Middle East Conflict
item.description: Even with gbege from the Middle East war, Emirates Group, wey be di owner of di world's biggest long-haul airline, don report say dem make $5.7 billion profit this year, a 3% increase from last year. ...

item.title: Two passengers self-isolate in UK following possible hantavirus exposure on MV Hondius cruise ship
item.description: Two individuals who recently returned to the UK from the MV Hondius cruise ship, currently affected by a hantavirus outbreak, have been instructed to self-isolate. UK Health Security Agency (UKHSA) of...

item.title: Government Moves to Revitalise TAZARA Railway for Better Cargo Transport in Southern Corridor
item.description: The Tanzania-Zambia Railway Authority (TAZARA) rehabilitation project is progressing well and it is expected to greatly improve cargo transportation along the important corridor. Deputy Minister for T...

item.title: Government introduces policy requiring 20% of new gas production to be sold domestically
item.description: The Albanese Government has announced a national gas reservation policy requiring Queensland’s three main LNG exporters to sell 20 per cent of any new gas they produce into the domestic market from Ju...
